result.summary: The page is generally recognizable, but there are several visual/layout problems: content is clipped horizontally, the active bottom navigation state appears inconsistent with the page content, and the fixed bottom nav overlaps the game grid.

issues.description: Multiple horizontal sections are clipped on the right edge. The top category tab shows only part of “Hot Games,” and game cards in the third column are partially cut off, suggesting the content is wider than the viewport or the carousel layout lacks clear containment.
issues.suggestion: Check mobile widths, grid/card sizing, gaps, and overflow behavior. If these are carousels, ensure the clipping is intentional and provide clear horizontal scroll affordance; otherwise fit the cards within the viewport.

issues.description: The bottom navigation highlights “POKER” as the active section, but the visible page content is casino-style game content with “CASINO” appearing as the relevant section. This creates a confusing active state mismatch.
issues.suggestion: Update the active bottom navigation item to match the current page, likely “CASINO,” or ensure the page content matches the selected “POKER” section.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation overlaps the lower game cards, cutting off visible content near the bottom of the screen.
issues.suggestion: Add sufficient bottom padding or safe-area spacing to the scrollable content so cards are not hidden behind the fixed navigation bar.

issues.description: Some carousel/category items appear abruptly truncated at the viewport edge, especially the “Hot Games” tab and rightmost game cards, which can look broken rather than intentionally scrollable.
issues.suggestion: Use fade indicators, partial-card spacing, or scroll hints to make horizontal scrolling feel intentional and polished.
